reviewer.name: OpenAI Codex
reviewer.responsibility: Claim extraction, primary-source mapping, scope correction, and verification design

sources.title: Event
sources.versionScope: Current DOM event model and evergreen browsers

sources.title: EventTarget.addEventListener()
sources.versionScope: Current DOM event model and evergreen browsers

claims.statement: A DOM event represents an occurrence that can be dispatched to an event target.
claims.applicability: Current DOM event model; Evergreen browsers

claims.statement: addEventListener registers a callback for a named event type on an EventTarget.
claims.applicability: Current DOM event model; Evergreen browsers

claims.statement: Event propagation and default browser actions are separate from listener execution.
claims.applicability: Current DOM event model; Evergreen browsers

verifications.method: Trigger the event once and confirm the intended handler runs once.
Inspect the event target or submitted values used by the handler.
verifications.expectedResult: The check exposes whether event behaves as documented: Inspect the event target or submitted values used by the handler.

verifications.method: Check whether browser default behavior should continue or be explicitly prevented.
verifications.expectedResult: The check exposes whether event behaves as documented: Check whether browser default behavior should continue or be explicitly prevented.

limitations: Events may be generated by user interaction, browser activity, or code.
Propagation, cancelability, and default actions vary by event type.
Registering a listener does not guarantee it runs only once; options and removal behavior matter.
Independent human review remains required before promotion to gold.
